mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing equity risk premium and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved corporate earnings expectations and lower default risk.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137684.0 as of t-1mo suggests ongoing labor market strength
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: Payroll growth remains above 150k persons monthly but 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of observations, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for 2+ months, the tightening cycle compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced credit availability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of t-1mo indicates restrictive monetary stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy rates
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with housing starts (HOUST) above trend indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity. Joint expansion in these series signals healthy aggregate demand, driving positive equity returns over 20 trading days through improved corporate revenue expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 372034.0 and HOUST at 1518.0 as of t-1mo and t-2mo respectively
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Both RSAFS and HOUST grow month-over-month but 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of co-expansion periods, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. This contraction triggers equity market repricing downward over 5 trading days as investors reduce growth expectations.
regime_hint: supply shock or cyclical downturn regime
failure_condition: INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month but 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, signaling labor market deterioration. This deterioration reduces consumer confidence and spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through lower corporate earnings.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months but 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 3%, creating a negative real rate environment. This regime compresses equity risk premiums through currency debasement concerns and forces portfolio reallocation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors seek real asset hedges.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 205.999 and FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of t-1mo
regime_hint: negative real rates with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: Negative real rate regime (CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% and FEDFUNDS < 3%) but 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle slowdown. This deceleration triggers equity market repricing downward over 60 trading days as investors reduce long-term earnings growth forecasts and shift to defensive positioning.
failure_condition: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2% but 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) remains stable or declines, indicating synchronized supply and labor expansion. This joint signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through improved profit margins and production capacity utilization.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 112.4866 and UNRATE at 4.5% as of t-2mo and t-1mo
regime_hint: synchronized expansion with supply-side productivity gains
failure_condition: INDPRO grows > 0.3% month-over-month with stable UNRATE but 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.
